preFix.title: Pregnancy-safe body oil
preFix.subtitle: A gentle unscented or lightly lavender-scented body oil for use during pregnancy
preFix.excerpt: This blend uses only carrier oils known to be safe throughout pregnancy, with lavender essential oil at a conservative 0.5% dilution: a rate considered safe from the second trimester onwards. Sweet almond oil and fractionated coconut oil are light, quickly absorbed, and gentle on sensitive skin.
preFix.sourceNotes: EO safety in pregnancy from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), chapter on reproductive safety. Sweet almond oil properties from cosmetic supplier datasheet. Lavender EO pregnancy caution from Tisserand Institute (tisserandinstitute.org, May 2026).

content.text: During pregnancy, the list of essential oils to avoid is long and the research on specific risk levels is limited. The safest approach is to keep any essential oil use minimal and restricted to oils with good safety records. Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) has one of the longest records of safe use in pregnancy at low dilutions: 0.5% is the conservative standard. Avoid the first trimester entirely for any scented product. This formula is also fully effective as an unscented
oil: simply omit the lavender for a scent-free version.
Sweet almond oil contains a tree-nut allergen. For those with tree-nut allergies, substitute an equal weight of fractionated coconut oil or jojoba oil, both of which are nut-free.

items.name: Sweet almond oil
items.prepNote: Light, skin-conditioning carrier; contains tree-nut allergen

items.name: Vitamin E oil
items.prepNote: Antioxidant; extends shelf life and conditions the skin

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: 0.5% dilution; Lavandula angustifolia only; from second trimester onwards; omit in first trimester

content.text: Combine all ingredients in a small jug and stir gently. Pour into a pump bottle or a small bottle with a flip top. Label with the date and the EO details if scented. Apply to damp skin after showering, working in sections and allowing the oil to absorb before dressing. For use on the abdomen, apply in gentle circular movements.

12 months at room temperature away from direct sunlight. The sweet almond oil is the stability-limiting ingredient. Discard if the oil smells sharp or rancid. No water content means no preservative is required.

initialFindings.message: paragraph reads at grade 11.2 (qc threshold: 11) — simplify (target grade 6-8)
